subject: Databases
title: One-to-Many Relationships
description: Learn One-to-Many Relationships in one minute.

hook.text: A customer has many orders. Each order belongs to one customer. The most common relationship in databases.

buildup.text: The 'many' side holds the foreign key: orders.customer_id references customers.id.

discovery.text: Posts and comments, departments and employees, categories and products — all one-to-many.

twist.text: Put the foreign key on the wrong side and you get one order with many customers. Direction matters.

climax.text: Always ask: which side is 'many'? That table gets the foreign key column.

punchline.text: One-to-many: the foreign key lives on the many side. Always.

quiz.question: In a one-to-many relationship, which table holds the foreign key?
options: The table on the 'many' side of the relationship
Both tables hold a foreign key to each other
